top_categories.workout_context: 77 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Argentina's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
Gran Fondo Argentina's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Argentina. Train it until it feels boring.
Gran Fondo Argentina's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 77 miles.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Gran Fondo Argentina's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Argentina. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At Gran Fondo Argentina, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.
At Gran Fondo Argentina, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 77 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Argentina’s 77 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 77 miles of mixed terrain in Buenos Aires, Argentina.
